message:
fix(runtime): revert event capacity capping, deduplicate turn_with via try_turn_with, fix epoll resize overflow

Reactor Events: reverts the previous capacity-capping push() back to
unbounded growth. Dropping events on capacity is fatal for edge-triggered
notifications (epoll ET) — a missed event means a socket never gets
polled again. The reactor's poll batch size is the correct place to
enforce limits, not the event buffer itself. Capacity now tracks the
actual Vec capacity as a hint rather than a hard limit.

IoDriver turn_with: eliminated the duplicated poll-dispatch logic by
delegating to try_turn_with and unwrapping the Option (None → 0). This
removes ~30 lines of duplicated reactor interaction code and ensures
both paths share the same contention-aware polling behavior.

Epoll reactor: fixed should_resize_poll_events to use checked_mul(4)
instead of saturating_mul(4) to avoid false resize triggers at extreme
usize values. Tests updated to reflect correct behavior: events are
no longer dropped at capacity, and near-max target values no longer
cause spurious resizes.

Lab reactor test: updated capacity-saturation test assertions to match
the new always-preserve semantics (count=2, not count=1 for 2 events
into a capacity-1 buffer).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
